title: Author ShowcaseShellViewModel
blocks: 11-author-shell-view-and-root-chrome-task (the ui task that renders this view model)
description: Create the view model that drives the top-level shell of the Turbo Widgets Shop example app. The shell is the only always-mounted screen in the app; it knows which route is currently showing, which aisle is currently selected on the nav chrome, and which product is currently opened (if any). Every piece of mutable shell state lives on reactive cells in this view model — never on the view.

context.shell_purpose: The shell is the outer frame of the app. It decides which inner showcase view to render and it supplies the contextual navigation chrome (top app bar + bottom/side nav) that wraps every showcase view.
context.navigation_cases: When the user is on shop_home, the aisle rail shows 'Shop' selected. When on an aisle detail, that aisle is highlighted. When on a product detail, the product's aisle is highlighted and a back button returns to the aisle detail.
context.responsive_behaviour: On mobile the nav lives in a bottom bar; on desktop it lives in a side rail. The view model does NOT need to know about device type — TResponsiveBuilder at the root decides that. The view model only knows about routes and selections.
context.theme_integration: The app bar hosts a sun/moon toggle. The view model does NOT own the brightness state — ThemeService already owns it. The view model exposes a method that delegates to the theme service.

goals: A ShowcaseShellViewModel class extending TBaseViewModel lives at example/lib/shell/views/showcase_shell/showcase_shell_view_model.dart.
The view model exposes the current route as a read-only ValueListenable<ShowcaseRoute>, backed by a TNotifier.
The view model exposes the currently-selected aisle key and currently-opened product key as read-only ValueListenables backed by TNotifiers.
Public methods allow the view to: navigate to shop home, navigate to an aisle, open a product detail, go back to the previous route, and toggle the theme.
The view model consumes ShowcaseCatalogService and ThemeService via constructor injection — no globals are reached for.
Disposal tears down all notifiers cleanly.
non_goals: Rendering anything — this task is pure state.
Owning the brightness value (ThemeService owns that).
Owning per-showcase-view state (each product has its own view model).
Knowing anything about device type or layout.
acceptance_criteria: All mutable state is held in private TNotifier fields; the view receives only read-only ValueListenables.
Constructor accepts ShowcaseCatalogService and ThemeService as required parameters.
Calling openProduct(route) updates both currentRoute and selectedProductKey in a single observable event where practical.
Calling goBack from a product detail returns the app to the aisle it came from; from an aisle detail returns to shop home; from shop home is a no-op.
toggleTheme delegates to the theme service, not to any internal field.
Dispose is idempotent and all listeners are detached.
constraints: Follow dev-conventions/views-and-view-models.md, dev-conventions/state.md, dev-conventions/mvvm.md, and dev-conventions/class-structure.md.
No setState, no ChangeNotifier raw usage — reactive state goes through TNotifier only.
No business logic for individual showcases — shell state is about routing and chrome selection only.
No direct access to SharedPreferences, Firebase, or any persistence layer — state is in-memory only.
tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP for reading existing turbo_mvvm TBaseViewModel and turbo_notifiers TNotifier implementations.

testing_and_verification.unit_tests: Write Gherkin-style unit tests verifying navigation state transitions: opening a product updates currentRoute and selectedProductKey; goBack pops correctly from each route level; navigating to an aisle resets selectedProductKey. No mocks — pass real ShowcaseCatalogService and a minimal ThemeService fake constructed from a TNotifier<Brightness>. Test observable ValueListenable outputs, not internal fields. Do not test theme toggling behaviour — that is ThemeService's responsibility.

quality_checklist.maintainability: Adding a new top-level route should require touching only the enum and this view model.
quality_checklist.dry: Do not duplicate routing logic that lives in ShowcaseRouter.
quality_checklist.single_responsibility: This view model owns routing and chrome selection. Nothing else.
quality_checklist.performance: TNotifier updates are O(1). No repeated allocations in method calls.

workflow_instructions: When done: `status` → `done`, add `worklog`, move file to `workspace/tasks/archive/dev/03-author-shell-view-model-task.json`.
Add `follow_up_notes` to 11-author-shell-view-and-root-chrome-task with the exact public API signature of this view model so the ui task can consume it without re-reading source.

worklog.summary: Created ShowcaseShellViewModel with reactive navigation state, constructor-injected services, and full Gherkin-style test coverage.
changes: Created shell/views/showcase_shell/showcase_shell_view_model.dart: TBaseViewModel<void> with TNotifier<ShowcaseRoute> currentRoute, TNotifier<String?> selectedAisleKey, TNotifier<String?> selectedProductKey
Public mutators: navigateToShopHome(), navigateToAisle(String), openProduct(ShowcaseRoute), goBack(), toggleTheme()
Read-only ValueListenable fetchers for all state + brightness delegation to ThemeService
Created test/shell/views/showcase_shell/showcase_shell_view_model_test.dart: 24 Gherkin-style tests covering all navigation transitions, no-op edge cases, reactive notifications, and theme delegation

follow_up_notes: Phase 2 created the services this view model consumes. Exact import paths:
DI pattern: each service has static `locate` getter and `registerLazySingleton()` method using GetIt. LocatorService.registerInitialDependencies() calls all registrations.
ThemeService exposes ValueListenable<Brightness> brightness and void toggleBrightness().
ShowcaseCatalogService exposes List<ShowcaseEntry> entries, byAisle(String), byRoute(ShowcaseRoute), static aisleLabel/aisleTagline helpers.
